signature.description: Save your signature so you don't have to redraw it every time you approve.
signature.add_subtitle: Draw or upload a signature image
signature.note: Signatures are saved as an image and embedded in the final PDF. This is not a PKI-certified digital signature, but it is protected by an audit trail.

settings_reminders.heading: Reminder Settings
settings_reminders.description: Configure email reminder intervals per approval level.
settings_reminders.fallback_hint: Used if a level has no individual configuration
settings_reminders.info_text: Reminders are sent automatically every working day (Monday–Friday) at 08:00 WIB to approvers with pending documents. Aviat Admin always receives a copy. The Global configuration acts as a fallback for levels without their own settings.

documents_create.signature_info_body: After submitting, you will be redirected to the document detail page. There you can use the drag-and-drop interface to place the signature and approver name box directly on the PDF page 1 preview.

help.heading: Help Center
help.description: Frequently asked questions and step-by-step guides for your role.

aviat.q1: What is the difference between Super Admin, Admin, and Viewer roles?
aviat.a1: Super Admin has full access including user and settings management. Admin can manage partners, documents, and templates. Viewer has read-only access and cannot create or modify data.
aviat.q2: How do I invite and manage Partners?
aviat.a2: Go to Partners in the sidebar, click Add Partner, fill in the company details, and save. An invitation email will be sent automatically to the partner's admin email.
aviat.q3: How do I create and clone a SOW Template with approval levels?
aviat.a3: Go to Templates/SOW, click Create Template, define the approval levels (L1, L2, etc.) with the responsible approvers, and save. To clone, open an existing template and use the Clone action.
aviat.q4: How do I add a new user and resend their invitation?
aviat.a4: Go to Users, click Add User, fill in the details and assign a role, then save. To resend an invitation, find the user in the list and click Resend Invitation.
aviat.q5: How do I monitor active documents and export a report?
aviat.a5: Go to Documents to see all documents with their current status. Use the filters to narrow down results, then click Export to download the data as an Excel file.
aviat.q6: Why are some menu items not visible for the Viewer role?
aviat.a6: The Viewer role is read-only. Menus for creating or modifying data (such as creating documents or managing users) are hidden to prevent unintended changes.
aviat.q7: How do reminder notifications work and how do I configure them?
aviat.a7: Reminder notifications are sent automatically to approvers when a document has been waiting for approval beyond the configured interval. Go to Settings → Setting Reminder (Super Admin only) to set the number of days per approval level and toggle weekday-only mode on or off.

partner.q1: How do I submit a new document?
partner.a1: Click Submit Document in the sidebar or on the dashboard. Select the document type, fill in the required fields, attach the necessary files, and submit.
partner.q2: What formats and attachments are required when submitting?
partner.a2: PDF is the required format for the main document. Supporting attachments (photos, reports) can be uploaded as PDF, JPG, or PNG. Check the template requirements for the specific document type.
partner.q3: How do I check the status of my document?
partner.a3: Go to My Documents in the sidebar. Each document shows its current status (e.g., Pending, Under Review, Approved, Rejected). Click a document to see detailed progress.
partner.q4: What should I do if my document is rejected or requires a revision (punchlist)?
partner.a4: You will receive a notification when a revision is requested. Open the document, review the punchlist comments from the approver, make the necessary corrections, and re-upload.
partner.q5: How do I upload a document revision?
partner.a5: Open the document that requires revision, scroll to the Revision section, upload the corrected file, and submit. The document will re-enter the approval queue.

approver.q1: How do I see documents waiting for my approval?
approver.a1: Click Need Approval in the sidebar. This list shows only documents assigned to you at your approval level that are currently waiting for action.
approver.q2: How do I approve or reject a document?
approver.a2: Open the document from the Need Approval list. Review the content and attachments, then click Approve to accept or Reject to decline. You can add a comment before confirming.
approver.q3: How do I attach my digital signature?
approver.a3: When approving, the system will prompt you to select a saved signature. If you have not saved one yet, go to your Profile → Saved Signature to upload it first.
approver.q4: What is multi-level approval and how does the order work?
approver.a4: Documents go through sequential approval levels (L1 → L2 → etc.) as defined in the template. Each level must be approved before the next approver is notified. You only act at your assigned level.
approver.q5: Where can I see my approval history?
approver.a5: Click History in the sidebar to see all documents you have previously approved or rejected, along with the date and any comments.

s1.title: Inviting & Managing Partners
s1.step1: Go to Partners in the sidebar.
s1.step2: Click Add Partner and fill in the company name, email, region, and PIC information.
s1.step3: Click Save. An invitation email is sent automatically to the partner admin.
s1.step4: To edit or deactivate a partner, find them in the list and click Edit.

s2.title: Creating a SOW Template & Approval Levels
s2.step1: Go to Templates/SOW and click Create Template.
s2.step2: Define the template name, document type, and add approval levels (e.g., L1 MS BO, L2 XLS RTH).
s2.step3: Assign the responsible approver role for each level and save.

s3.title: Managing Users
s3.step1: Go to Users and click Add User.
s3.step2: Fill in the user's name, email, and assign a role. Click Save.
s3.step3: To resend an invitation, find the user in the list and click Resend Invitation.

s4.title: Monitoring & Exporting Documents
s4.step1: Go to Documents to view all documents and their current statuses.
s4.step2: Use filters (status, partner, date range) to narrow down the list.
s4.step3: Click Export to download the filtered results as an Excel file.

s5.title: Submit New Request (New Document)
s5.step1: Go to Documents and click Submit Document (or Create).
s5.step2: Select the Partner, Template/SOW type, fill in the contract details (number, scope, dates), and assign the PIC.
s5.step3: Upload the required PDF document and any supporting attachments.
s5.step4: Click Submit. The document enters the approval queue at L1 and the relevant approver is notified.

s6.title: Submit Ongoing Document (Import)
s6.step1: Go to Documents and click Import.
s6.step2: Fill in the document details including current approval stage and status to reflect the real-world progress.
s6.step4: Click Submit. The document is recorded in the system at the indicated stage, bypassing earlier approval levels.

s7.title: Reassign Document
s7.step1: Open the document that needs to be reassigned from the Documents list.
s7.step2: Click the Reassign action (available to Admin and Super Admin).
s7.step3: Select the new responsible approver or partner PIC and confirm. The document workflow continues from the current stage under the new assignee.

s8.title: Setting Reminder
s8.step1: Go to Settings → Setting Reminder in the sidebar (Super Admin only).
s8.step2: For each approval level (L1, L2, etc.) set the number of days after which a reminder email is sent to the approver.
s8.step3: Toggle Weekday Only on to restrict reminders to business days (Monday–Friday). Click Save.

s9.title: L1 Approval (Admin Review)
s9.step1: Click L1 Approvals in the sidebar. The badge shows the number of documents waiting.
s9.step2: Open a document to review its content, attached files, and submission details.
s9.step3: Click Approve to forward the document to the next approval level, or Reject to return it to the partner.
s9.step4: Add a comment if needed, then confirm. The partner and next approver are notified automatically.

s1.title: Submitting a New Document
s1.step1: Click Submit Document in the sidebar or the dashboard shortcut.
s1.step2: Select the document type and fill in all required fields (contract number, scope, dates).
s1.step3: Upload the main PDF and any required supporting attachments.
s1.step4: Click Submit. You will receive a confirmation and the document will enter the approval queue.

s2.title: Tracking Status in My Documents
s2.step1: Click My Documents in the sidebar.
s2.step2: Each row shows the document name, submission date, and current status.
s2.step3: Click a document to see the full approval progress, including which level is currently active.

s3.title: Handling a Revision / Punchlist
s3.step1: You will receive a notification when a revision is requested. Click the notification to open the document.
s3.step2: Review the punchlist comments left by the approver explaining what needs to be corrected.
s3.step3: Upload the corrected file in the Revision section and click Submit Revision.

s1.title: Reviewing the Need Approval Queue
s1.step1: Click Need Approval in the sidebar to see all documents assigned to you.
s1.step2: The badge number shows how many documents are waiting for your action.
s1.step3: Click a document to open the detail view and review all content and attachments.

s2.title: Approving or Rejecting with a Signature
s2.step1: Open a document from the Need Approval list.
s2.step2: Review all tabs (document details, attachments, audit trail).
s2.step3: Click Approve or Reject. If approving, select your saved signature from the dropdown.
s2.step4: Optionally add a comment, then confirm. The document moves to the next approval level or is completed.

s3.title: Viewing Approval History
s3.step2: Browse all documents you have previously approved or rejected, with timestamps and your comments.

idle.title: Session About to Expire
idle.description: You have been inactive for a while. You will be logged out automatically if no action is taken.

get_started.title: Welcome to Acceptra
get_started.description: Get familiar with the Acceptra workflow through the User Guide, or check the FAQ if you have questions.
